editorial_angle: Google just published a protocol that treats your UI as an API for AI agents. Not accessibility. Not automation. A first-class citizen spec for machines navigating interfaces built for humans. The deeper story: we're entering a world where UIs have two audiences — humans and agents — and nobody's figured out what happens when those audiences want different things. This isn't the agentic web arriving. It's the moment someone tried to write the spec for it.
key_message: Google's A2UI protocol means the 'user' in user interface is no longer assumed to be human.

visual_metaphor_direction.core_tension: A human hand and a robotic arm both reaching for the same UI control — the interface was designed for one, now claimed by both. Neither fully owns it.
key_elements: Google logo or 'G' branding on the interface or screen
A physical control panel or dashboard with human-centric affordances: buttons, dials, labeled inputs
A mechanical or skeletal robot arm reaching in from one side, finger extended toward a control
A human hand reaching from the other side, slightly displaced or uncertain
A placard or label reading 'USER' that has been partially relabeled or overlaid with 'AGENT'
avoid: Friendly robots or cute AI mascots — this is not a consumer product launch
Abstract neural network imagery, glowing nodes, or digital particle effects
Smartphones or laptops as the primary interface device — too generic
Any sense of triumph or celebration — the mood is unresolved curiosity, not victory
Dystopian darkness or threat — surreal and curious, not ominous

x.hook: The 'user' in UI might not be you anymore.
x.angle: Google just published a protocol for AI agents to navigate UIs. That's not a feature. That's a new primary audience.

linkedin.hook: Your product has two users now. One of them is an AI.
linkedin.angle: Google's A2UI spec formalizes something product teams have been ignoring: agents are using your interfaces, and they weren't designed for them. What does it actually mean to do UX for a machine?

bluesky.hook: Google dropped a spec for AI agents to navigate UIs and I can't stop thinking about it.
bluesky.angle: A2UI is basically: here's how an agent should understand a button. Fifty years of designing UIs for human perception and now we're writing the machine-readable version. Weird moment to be paying attention.
